title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pulchella
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pulchella, also known as kidney vetch, is a flowering plant species in the legume family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which contribute to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of kidney vetch include its potential to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Kidney vetch is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal properties.
